name: C3 Allocator API
summary: C3's heap+temp split with an `@pool` scoped temp-flush macro and a `VMEM_TEMP` debug mode that turns escaped temp pointers into deterministic segfaults via virtual-memory protection.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
C3 is close to Odin in spirit but more explicit about the temporary-allocation idiom. Heap allocation goes through `mem`, temp allocation through `tmem`, and an `@pool` macro flushes temp allocations at the end of a lexical scope. Many APIs have `t`/`temp_` variants (`tmalloc`, `tnew`, `string::tformat`) that route into `tmem`.

The reusable lesson C3 contributes: arena-style temp allocation becomes much safer when paired with a debug mode that makes escaped temp pointers crash deterministically.

sections.title: VMEM_TEMP and TrackingAllocator
sections.content:
`VMEM_TEMP` uses virtual-memory protection so use-after-pool segfaults deterministically — when an `@pool` exits, its arena pages are `mprotect`'d unreadable, and any escaped pointer dereference traps. `TrackingAllocator` records leaks and backtraces. The combination means temp-arena bugs are caught at the point of misuse rather than through silent corruption later.

This pairs nicely with the `tmalloc`/`tnew`/`tformat` idiom: programmers can lean on temp allocation freely, knowing that escaped pointers will trap rather than leak into the next pool's allocations.
